exact_cause.details: _tracked_open_assets() was returning an empty mapping in the live runtime because the current runtime discovery cache did not yet provide token IDs for the open-position markets. With no tracked asset IDs, the market websocket had nothing to subscribe to, so no live marks could enter state_hub.

proof_of_work.open_positions_inspected: Current open trades were present in _fetch_trades(), so the problem was not absence of open positions.
proof_of_work.discovery_cache_check: runtime/last_discovery.json existed, but the live runtime mapping initially produced no usable token-id subscriptions for tracked open positions.
proof_of_work.after_refresh_token_proof: After a discovery refresh, runtime/last_discovery.json contained yes_token_id/no_token_id for the open-position slugs, and _tracked_open_assets() returned non-empty tracked asset mappings.

exact_token_ids_inspected.result: After refresh, these slugs resolved to concrete yes_token_id / no_token_id values.

subscribe_payload_diagnosis.status: payload logic itself was not the first blocker because tracked asset mapping was empty at runtime before refresh.

parser_statehub_diagnosis.details: Parser/state_hub were not the first confirmed failure point. The first confirmed failure was zero tracked assets to subscribe.

runtime_action_needed_next: Refresh discovery cache in the active runtime path and restart/reload the dashboard server so the live process subscribes using the now-persisted token IDs. Then re-test live mark ingress.
